mission.title: flux: Ingress docs for Flux webhook receiver are missing important details
mission.description: Ingress docs for Flux webhook receiver are missing important details. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Ingress docs for Flux webhook receiver are missing important details

steps.title: Check current Ingress configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux resources:
```bash
kubectl get ingress -A
kubectl describe ingress <name> -n <namespace>
```
### Describe the bug

I wrote parts of these webhook receiver docs, in "[Expose the webhook receiver](https://fluxcd.io/docs/guides/webhook-receivers/#expose-the-webhook-receiver)" I mentioned cert-ma

steps.title: Apply the fix for Ingress docs for Flux webhook receiver are missing important
steps.description:
Fixes fluxcd/flux2#2240

And some other minor issues I found while running through the webhook receiver guide:

* A tab character at the end of a copy-paste block that fouls things up
* The name of the receiver service is misnamed above a copy paste block
```yaml
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: webhook-receiver
  namespace: flux-system
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt
spec:
  rules:
    - host: fluxwebhook.example
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: webhook-receiver
                port:
                  number: 80
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - fluxwebhook.example
      secretName: webhook-receiver-https
---
kind: NetworkPolicy
apiVersion: networking.k8s.i
```

steps.title: Confirm Ingress docs for Flux webhook receiver are missing is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Fixes fluxcd/flux2#2240

And some other minor issues I found while running through the webhook receiver guide:

* A tab character at the end of a copy-paste block that fouls things up
* The name of the receiver service is misnamed above a copy paste block. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: webhook-receiver
  namespace: flux-system
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt
spec:
  rules:
    - host: fluxwebhook.example
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: webhook-receiver
                port:
                  number: 80
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - fluxwebhook.example
      secretName: webhook-receiver-https
---
kind: NetworkPolicy
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
metadata:
  name: allow-cert-manager-resolver-reverse
  namespace: cert-manager
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: cert-manager
  egress:
    - to:

---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: NetworkPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-cert-manager-http-solver
  namespace: flux-system
spec:
  policyTypes:
    - Ingress
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      acme.cert-manager.io/http01-solver: "true"
  ingress:
    - {}

cert-manager   allow-cert-manager-resolver-reverse   app.kubernetes.io/instance=cert-manager   116m
flux-system    allow-cert-manager-resolver           acme.cert-manager.io/http01-solver=true   116m

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
